examples.title: Huffman Coding Algorithm
examples.description: Greedy algorithm to build optimal prefix codes
examples.explanation: The algorithm uses a min-heap to repeatedly extract the two nodes with minimum frequency, creates an internal node with their sum, and inserts it back. This greedy approach ensures optimal weighted path length.

examples.title: Huffman Decoding Algorithm
examples.explanation: Start at root, follow 0 for left and 1 for right. When a leaf is reached, output the character and return to root. Since codes are prefix-free, decoding is unambiguous.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m) where m = length of encoded bits

examples.title: Optimal Merge Pattern using Huffman
examples.explanation: This is exactly the Huffman algorithm applied to the optimal merge pattern problem. The greedy choice of always merging the two smallest files first minimizes the total cost of all merges.
